conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, let me review the official eligibility rules to ensure I give you accurate guidance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now I can help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To get started, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children under the age of 16, or children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who is 13 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 13 years old.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few more questions:

**Do you live with Alex in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK - I live abroad. But Alex does live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. You live abroad with Alex.

**Which country do you currently live in?** (This is important because you may still be eligible for Child Benefit if you live in certain countries, for example if you're a Crown servant posted abroad or living in certain countries with reciprocal agreements.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live in Spain at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. So you and Alex live in Spain.

**Are you a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of HM Forces) posted abroad, or are you working for the UK government overseas?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or working for the UK government.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

**Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess your eligibility based on what you've told me.

Based on the official UK government guidance, I now have enough information to make a determination about your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete**

I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

While Alex is 13 years old and meets the age requirement (under 16), the key eligibility rule is that you normally must live in the UK to qualify for Child Benefit. 

You may be able to claim while living abroad only if:
- You're a Crown servant (diplomat, HM Forces member, etc.) posted abroad, OR
- You have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme and meet certain conditions

Since you live in Spain and are not a Crown servant or working for the UK government, and you don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, you don't meet the residence requirement for Child Benefit.

If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you return to live in the UK), you may become eligible at that time.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old, which meets the age requirement (under 16). However, according to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 AND you live in the UK. The rules state you may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries (such as being a Crown servant posted abroad), but you have confirmed you are not a Crown servant or working for the UK government. You also do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Since you live in Spain and do not fall under any of the exceptions that would allow you to claim Child Benefit while living abroad, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in Spain with their 13-year-old child Alex. While Alex meets the age requirement for Child Benefit, the claimant does not meet the residence requirement. The claimant is not a Crown servant, does not work for the UK government overseas, and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Therefore, they do not qualify for Child Benefit under the current rules as they do not live in the UK and do not fall under any of the exceptions for living abroad.

However, according to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 AND you live in the UK. The rules state you may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries (such as being a Crown servant posted abroad), but you have confirmed you are not a Crown servant or working for the UK government. You also do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Since you live in Spain and do not fall under any of the exceptions that would allow you to claim Child Benefit while living abroad, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in Spain with their 13-year-old child Alex. While Alex meets the age requirement for Child Benefit, the claimant does not meet the residence requirement. The claimant is not a Crown servant, does not work for the UK government overseas, and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Therefore, they do not qualify for Child Benefit under the current rules as they do not live in the UK and do not fall under any of the exceptions for living abroad.
